data.title: 3 Polish Stocks On The Radar
data.description:
Poland is becoming harder for investors to ignore.

As companies across Europe rethink supply chains, manufacturing footprints and logistics strategy, Poland is emerging as one of the region's most important markets for long term industrial and infrastructure exposure.

The country's investment case is being driven by more than low costs. Poland offers a mix of strong domestic demand, rising foreign investment, nearshoring appeal and strategic logistics advantages, making it one of the more compelling structural growth stories in Europe.

That could keep names like CD Projekt, Orlen and KGHM Polska Miedź on investor watchlists as capital continues shifting toward Central Europe.

Why It Matters

Poland is no longer just a lower cost manufacturing market.

It is increasingly becoming a strategic European hub for:

  • Nearshoring
  • Industrial production
  • Warehouse expansion
  • Logistics infrastructure
  • Energy and materials exposure

That matters because investors looking across Europe are increasingly focused on markets that combine:

  • Relative economic resilience
  • Supply chain relevance
  • Skilled labor
  • Infrastructure investment
  • Long term corporate reinvestment

Poland checks many of those boxes.

Poland's Investment Case Is Built On Scale And Positioning

Poland's economy has become one of the more resilient stories in Europe, supported by strong domestic consumption and continued foreign direct investment.

Private consumption contributes roughly 57.8% of nominal GDP, a sign that the country's growth profile is not purely export driven.

Foreign direct investment also remains strong. In 2025, inflows reached roughly $33 billion, up nearly 15% year over year, while almost 94% of foreign companies reportedly reinvest profits locally.

That kind of reinvestment matters because it suggests foreign capital is not just entering Poland. It is staying there.

For investors, that strengthens the case that Poland is becoming a durable part of Europe's long term industrial map rather than a temporary cost advantage trade.

Nearshoring Could Be One Of Europe's Biggest Poland Trades

One of the strongest structural themes supporting Poland is nearshoring.

As manufacturers in Germany and the Benelux region look to bring parts of their supply chains closer to the European Union, Poland is increasingly being viewed as a preferred destination.

That makes sense.

The country sits at the center of major north south and east west trade corridors and borders seven countries, including Germany. It also offers:

  • More than 5,000 kilometers of expressways and motorways
  • Roughly 19,000 kilometers of modernized railways
  • Four deep sea ports
  • Fast access to major European consumer markets

For companies focused on speed, resilience and lower transport risk, that positioning is hard to ignore.

Warehousing And Logistics Could Stay In Focus

Poland's warehouse and logistics story is becoming one of the clearest ways to understand its structural growth.

By the end of 2025, modern warehouse stock in Poland exceeded 35 million square meters, up sharply from 7.1 million square meters in 2021.

Annual demand for new warehouse and logistics space reached around 6.8 million square meters, supported by:

  • E commerce growth
  • Omnichannel retail expansion
  • Regional distribution demand
  • Supply chain realignment

That makes Poland one of Europe's most important logistics markets, not just an emerging one.

For investors, even when listed direct plays are limited, the warehouse buildout supports broader themes in industrial demand, transport fuels, materials and infrastructure linked businesses.

Manufacturing Still Anchors The Story

Industry and logistics remain the backbone of Poland's economy.

The sector accounts for about 23% of GDP and employs more than 2.8 million people.

What makes Poland stand out is that it combines lower labor costs with improving productivity and a strong talent pipeline.

Manufacturing and logistics labor costs average around €14 per hour, still well below Western Europe, while educational attainment remains high and technical talent continues entering the workforce.

That matters because Poland is increasingly seen not just as a lower cost location, but as a more advanced industrial base tied to:

  • Machinery
  • Electronics
  • Vehicles
  • Battery production
  • Supply chain manufacturing

Exports of machinery, electronics and vehicles exceeded $135 billion in 2025, reinforcing Poland's role in European industrial supply chains.

3 Polish Stocks That Could Stay On The Radar

CD Projekt

The company benefits from a domestic ecosystem supported by:

  • A strong education pipeline
  • Roughly 80,000 IT graduates entering the market annually
  • Development costs still below Western Europe
  • A growing technology workforce

That makes CD Projekt more than a gaming name. It is also a visible play on Poland's digital economy and its long term creative and software export potential.

Orlen

In 2025, the group reported revenue above PLN 380 billion, or roughly $95 billion.

That scale matters because Poland's role as a transport and industrial hub supports demand tied to:

  • Fuel distribution
  • Logistics infrastructure
  • Industrial energy systems
  • Petrochemicals
  • Renewable investment

The company is also investing more than PLN 40 billion in petrochemical and renewable projects through 2027, which could keep it relevant as Poland balances industrial growth with energy transition spending.

KGHM Polska Miedź

KGHM (OTC:KGHPF) remains one of Poland's clearest materials and electrification linked names.

The company produced roughly 450,000 tonnes of copper in 2025 and reported revenue above $8.5 billion.

That keeps KGHM strategically relevant because copper remains critical to:

  • Power grids
  • Electrification
  • Industrial expansion
  • Infrastructure buildout
  • Manufacturing investment

As Europe continues building out supply chains tied to energy transition and industrial resilience, KGHM offers direct exposure to one of the region's most important input materials.

What Could Drive The Poland Trade

If Poland stays in focus as a strategic European market, investors will likely watch several key themes:

  • Whether nearshoring continues accelerating across Germany and Western Europe
  • Whether warehouse demand remains strong as supply chains shift
  • Whether industrial exports continue growing
  • Whether energy and materials names benefit from infrastructure spending
  • Whether Poland keeps attracting foreign capital at current levels

Poland's story is not built on one short term catalyst.

It is built on a combination of geography, labor quality, industrial depth and strategic relevance to Europe's next supply chain cycle.

Bottom Line

Poland is increasingly looking like one of Europe's more important long term investment destinations.

With strong domestic demand, rising foreign investment, growing logistics capacity and a clear role in nearshoring and industrial expansion, the country offers a more structural opportunity than many investors may realize.

For investors, the best ways to express that view may remain selective, with names tied to digital exports, industrial energy and electrification-linked materials.

That could keep CD Projekt, Orlen and KGHM Polska Miedź in focus as Poland's role in Europe continues to expand.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Real Brokerage To Acquire RE/MAX In $880 Million Deal
data.description:
The acquisition will create a leading technology-enabled global real estate platform.

Deal Terms

The deal values each RE/MAX Holdings share at $13.80, based on Real’s closing price on April 24, 2026.

Under the agreement, RE/MAX Holdings shareholders can choose either 5.152 shares of Real REMAX Group or $13.80 in cash. The cash payments are subject to proration, so the total cash consideration ranges from $60 million to $80 million.

Real shareholders will receive one share of the combined Real REMAX Group for each existing share held.

The companies expect the acquisition to close in the second half of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and approvals.

After closing, Real shareholders will own about 59% of the combined company, while RE/MAX Holdings shareholders will own roughly 41% on a fully diluted basis, assuming midpoint cash election.

As of December 31, Real’s cash and cash equivalents stood at $151.2 million.

Combined Company & Synergies

The deal is expected to enhance the combined company’s offerings, leveraging Real’s AI-powered brokerage platform alongside RE/MAX’s extensive brand recognition and global reach.

The companies aim to deliver a differentiated home buying and selling experience for over 180,000 real estate professionals and their clients across more than 120 countries and territories.

The combined company is projected to generate around $2.3 billion in annual revenue and $157 million in Adjusted EBITDA before synergies in 2025.

The transaction is expected to be accretive to Real’s earnings and EBITDA margin within the first full fiscal year following the close.

Real targets net debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA below 2.0x by the end of the second full year post-close, while still funding growth and technology investments.

The deal is also expected to deliver around $30 million in annual run-rate cost synergies, largely realized by 2027. These savings will come from shared services, corporate and public company costs, and technology efficiencies, and are expected to add roughly 100 basis points to margins once fully achieved.

What RE/MAX (RMAX) Does And Why The Real Deal Matters

RE/MAX Holdings operates as a franchisor of real estate brokerage services, focusing on recruiting and retaining agents while selling franchises. The company generates maximum revenue from its Real Estate segment, which is crucial for its overall performance.

The recent acquisition by Real Brokerage is significant as it combines two complementary business models, enhancing RE/MAX’s market position and expanding its technological capabilities. This merger is expected to create a stronger entity that can better serve its extensive network of agents and clients.

RE/MAX (RMAX) Earnings Preview And Analyst Price Targets

The countdown is on: RE/MAX Holdings is set to report earnings on May 7, 2026 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: 19 cents (Down from 24 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $73.01 million (Down from $74.47 million)
  • Valuation: P/E of 20.0x (Indicates fair valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold rating with an average price target of $27.05. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Goldman Sachs: Upgraded to Buy (Raised Target to $30.00) (March 25)
  • Morgan Stanley: Maintained Equal-Weight (Target $25.00) (March 15)
  • JP Morgan: Downgraded to Neutral (Target $22.00) (March 10)

Price Action: RE/MAX Holdings shares were up 20.57% at $9.63 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data. Real Brokerage shares were down 27.24%.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: POET Technologies Shares Plunge On Marvell Order Cancellation: What Investors Need To Know
data.description:
The cancellation notice, received on April 23, effectively terminates a partnership that included initial production units first disclosed by POET in April 2023. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • POET Technologies shares are sliding. What’s weighing on POET shares?

Marvell Alleges Confidentiality Breach In POET Order Cancellation

The justification for the move is particularly concerning: Marvell alleged that POET breached confidentiality obligations by disclosing sensitive details regarding purchase orders and shipping information.

While POET management aims to pivot toward other strategic priorities, including a recently announced $5 million purchase order with a separate technology firm, the market remains skeptical.

POET emphasized its continued development of its patented POET Optical Interposer platform for AI networks and hyperscale data centers. However, the company acknowledged significant risks, noting there is no assurance it can re-establish its relationship with Marvell.

Investors are weighing this loss of a key partner against the company's ability to meet its remaining revenue targets and satisfy existing obligations.

Critical Levels To Watch for POET Stock

POET is still trading near the upper end of its 52-week range ($3.78 to $15.50), which is consistent with a strong longer-term uptrend even after sharp pullbacks. The stock is trading 15.8% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 31.9% above its 100-day SMA, a setup that leans bullish on trend but can be prone to fast mean-reversion moves when sentiment flips.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 81.43, which is firmly overbought and often lines up with choppier trading. RSI at 81.43 means buyers have been in control, but it also raises the odds of profit-taking bursts.

  • Key Resistance: $9.00 — a prior ceiling area where rallies have previously stalled.
  • Key Support: $12.00 — a nearby "line in the sand" traders may watch after the premarket drop.

What Is POET Technologies and Its Business Model?

POET Technologies is focused on designing and developing high-speed optical engines, light source products, and custom optical modules aimed at AI systems and hyperscale data centers.

A core part of the story is its POET Optical Interposer, a patented platform designed to integrate electronic and photonic devices into a single chip using wafer-level semiconductor manufacturing techniques.

POET Technologies Earnings Preview For May 2026

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 13 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 5 cents (Down from 8 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: 35 cents million (Up from 17 cents million YoY)

POET Stock Price Activity During Premarket Trading

POET Stock Price Activity: POET Technologies shares were down 40.60% at $8.97 on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Greenland Energy Stock Soars 27% After Halliburton Deal
data.description:
• Greenland Energy stock is showing exceptional strength. What’s driving GLND stock higher?

Deal Highlights

The agreement covers integrated consulting services and logistical management, including planning, coordination, handling and transportation of equipment, services and goods, along with comprehensive well and drilling services for Greenland Energy's onshore campaign in the Jameson Land Basin.

Together with previously announced agreements with Stampede Drilling and Desgagnés, the company said these arrangements are intended to support the execution of its first onshore exploration well in the basin.

"Greenland Energy Company’s agreement with Halliburton is another pivotal milestone as we execute our 2026 drilling program and build on the momentum following our NASDAQ listing," said CEO Robert Price.

Operational Outlook

Greenland Energy expects to drill its first two wells in 2026 following more than a year of logistical planning and site preparation. The Jameson Land Basin spans approximately 2 million acres and includes multiple identified targets supported by historical and modern seismic data.

Greenland Energy Shares Race Higher

GLND Price Action: At the time of publication, Greenland Energy shares are trading 15.14% higher at $7.15, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

This illustration was generated using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.

data.title: Strategy Buys 3,273 Bitcoin For $255 Million: MSTR Targets $205
data.description:
The ATM Funding Shift

An SEC filing shows that Strategy funded the purchases entirely through the sale of common shares via its at-the-market equity offering program.

This marks a departure from recent purchases, as the company primarily financed those using proceeds from variable-rate preferred stock STRC.

Strategy now holds 818,334 BTC acquired for a cumulative $61.81 billion at an average cost basis of $75,537 per Bitcoin. 

Moreover, the company has bought Bitcoin every week in April, totalling over $6.4 billion in purchases this month alone.

Saylor Teased The Buy

Michael Saylor hinted at the purchase over the weekend. On Sunday, April 26, Saylor posted on X with the message “The ₿eat Goes On” alongside Strategy’s famous “Orange Dots” chart showing previous Bitcoin buys.

Last week, Strategy added 34,164 Bitcoin to its treasury at an average price of about $74,395 per coin for a total cost of roughly $2.54 billion, according to an April 20 filing. 

The aggressive accumulation continues despite ongoing criticism from gold advocate Peter Schiff, who has called STRC a “Ponzi scheme.”

The Cup And Handle Breakout

MSTR closed down 0.84% to $171.02 Friday, with premarket Monday at $171.58—essentially flat, which is resilient considering Bitcoin’s 1% pullback today.

A well-defined cup and handle pattern that formed from February's $80 low to the April neckline near $165 has now broken out. 

Last week's move above that neckline came with conviction, confirming the pattern and shifting the broader trend.

The 100 EMA at $165.01 has flipped into support, while the 20 EMA at $150.66 and 50 EMA at $146.04 continue to trend higher. The RSI at 65.79 is strong but not yet overbought. There’s room to run before this gets stretched.

On the levels, immediate support sits at $165.01, followed by $160 and then $150.66.

Resistance is now stacked at $177 to $180, with a breakout opening the path toward $205.82 near the 200 EMA.

Image: Shutterstock
